input.headline: Wales’ route to World Cup 2026 in full after play-off draw

raw.headline: Wales’ route to World Cup 2026 in full after play-off draw

winner: In the spirit of Welsh soccer, they'll be playing their way out instead of into the World Cup 2026!

candidates.text: Why don't Welsh footballers play darts? Because they can never hit the target, even when it comes to World Cup qualifiers!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but relies on a generic stereotype about Welsh footballers and darts. It lacks specificity and surprise, and the connection to the World Cup qualifiers is weak."}

candidates.text: Why are Welsh football fans so excited about the play-offs for the World Cup 2026? Because they're finally getting a chance to draw with some big teams!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and uses a play on words with 'draw,' but it's a bit generic and lacks the surprise or depth needed for higher humor impact."}

candidates.text: In light of the news, Wales' route to the World Cup 2026 now includes a trip through their local park... just as they hoped.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, slightly surprising, and ties the headline to a humorous local twist. However, it lacks deeper specificity and could be more original."}

candidates.text: A Welsh sheep declared its intention to kick off the World Cup 2026 with a well-aimed woolen winkle ball.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the sheep and 'woolen winkle ball,' but it's a bit forced and lacks clear connection to the headline. The humor is mild and not strongly tied to the specific context of Wales' World Cup route."}

candidates.text: In the spirit of Welsh soccer, they'll be playing their way out instead of into the World Cup 2026!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and ties to Welsh soccer, but the humor is weak and relies on a vague pun that may not land well for most audiences."}
